hero.title: Know when your codebase drifts before it breaks
hero.subtitle: Cross-correlate signals from git, CI, dependencies, and deployments. Local-first, zero-config, evidence-based.

features.title: Why Evolution Engine
features.subtitle: One command gives you visibility that no single tool can provide.
features.local_first_desc: Your code never leaves your machine. All analysis runs locally. Only opt-in telemetry (anonymous usage stats) ever phones home.
features.zero_config_desc: Auto-detects your tools from configs, lockfiles, and imports. No YAML, no dashboards, no setup. Just evo analyze .
features.evidence_based_desc: Statistical deviation from your repo's own baseline. Modified z-scores, not arbitrary thresholds. Calibrated across 43 open-source repos.
features.cross_signal_desc: Correlates git + CI + deps + deploys. "CI fails after big dependency updates" is a pattern no single tool sees.
features.extensible_desc: Open adapter ecosystem. EE discovers available adapters for your tools, notifies you about updates, and auto-pulls community patterns.

how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.subtitle: Three steps. No configuration needed.
how_it_works.step1_desc: One pip install. Works with Python 3.10+.
how_it_works.step2_desc: Run in any git repo. EE auto-detects your tools and computes signals.
how_it_works.step3_desc: Read the advisory, investigate with AI, or set up the GitHub Action for continuous monitoring.

signals.title: Signal Families
signals.subtitle: EE ingests events from multiple data sources and computes deviation metrics for each.
signals.dependency_source: pip, npm, go, cargo, bundler lockfiles

adapters.title: Adapter Catalog
adapters.subtitle: Built-in adapters plus a growing plugin ecosystem. Each adapter reads from a data source and feeds it into the EE pipeline.
adapters.git_desc: Local git history analysis — commits, file changes, co-change patterns.
adapters.pip_desc: Python dependency tracking from requirements.txt and Pipfile.lock.
adapters.npm_desc: Node.js dependency tracking from package-lock.json with depth analysis.
adapters.go_desc: Go module tracking from go.sum with dependency depth.
adapters.cargo_desc: Rust crate tracking from Cargo.lock with depth analysis.
adapters.github_actions_desc: CI pipeline monitoring — run duration, failure rates, workflow trends.
adapters.github_releases_desc: Release cadence tracking, pre-release detection, asset monitoring.
adapters.dependabot_desc: Reads security advisory data from repos that use Dependabot alerts.
adapters.jenkins_desc: Reads CI build data from repos that use Jenkins.
adapters.gitlab_ci_desc: Reads CI/CD pipeline data from repos that use GitLab CI.
adapters.datadog_desc: Reads APM metrics, error rates, and deployment markers from repos that use Datadog.
adapters.sentry_desc: Reads error rates and release health data from repos that use Sentry.
adapters.sonarqube_desc: Reads quality gate status and code metrics from repos that use SonarQube.
adapters.snyk_desc: Reads vulnerability and license compliance data from repos that use Snyk.
adapters.pagerduty_desc: Reads incident frequency, MTTR, and escalation pattern data from teams that use PagerDuty.
adapters.jira_desc: Reads cycle time, WIP count, and sprint velocity from teams that use Jira.
adapters.argocd_desc: Reads GitOps deployment and sync status data from repos that use Argo CD.
adapters.build_your_own_desc: Create a custom adapter with our scaffold tool and step-by-step guide. Ship it as a pip package.
adapters.dont_see: Don't see your tool? We prioritize by community demand.

adapters_page.title: Adapter Ecosystem
adapters_page.subtitle: Built-in adapters, a plugin system for extensions, and an open ecosystem. Any tool with project data can become an adapter.
adapters_page.builtin_subtitle: Ships with Evolution Engine. Zero config, auto-detected from your repo.
adapters_page.openapi_desc: API schema change tracking from OpenAPI/Swagger specs.
adapters_page.docker_desc: Container config change tracking from Dockerfiles and compose files.
adapters_page.ci_local_desc: Local CI workflow analysis from GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, and CircleCI config files.
adapters_page.gitlab_pipelines_desc: GitLab CI/CD pipeline monitoring via API.
adapters_page.gitlab_releases_desc: GitLab release cadence tracking via API.
adapters_page.plugin_subtitle: Install any adapter via pip. Build your own in minutes. Plugins are auto-discovered at runtime.
adapters_page.install_desc: Third-party adapters are regular pip packages. Install one and EE discovers it automatically on the next run.
adapters_page.build_desc: Scaffold a new adapter in one command. Implement iter_events(), validate, and publish.
adapters_page.reference_desc: A working example adapter that reads test coverage data. Install it to see how plugins integrate with the pipeline.
adapters_page.ecosystem_subtitle: EE already detects these tools in your repo via prescan. Community adapters coming soon — or build one yourself.
adapters_page.open_ended: ...and any other tool with project data. The list above is non-exhaustive.
adapters_page.request_subtitle: We prioritize adapters by community demand. Tell us what you need, or build one yourself.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Start free. Upgrade when you need CI, deployment, and security signals.
pricing.free_desc: Forever free for git + dependency analysis
pricing.pro_desc: Full pipeline visibility with AI-powered investigation
pricing.billing_disclosure: Billed monthly. Cancel anytime. 30-day money-back guarantee.

consent.text: This site uses cookies for payment processing (Stripe) and optional anonymous telemetry. No tracking or advertising cookies are used.

footer.tagline: Your code never leaves your machine.

modal.title: Request an Adapter
modal.use_case_placeholder: What would this adapter help you detect or monitor?
modal.description_placeholder: API docs, data format, or anything else helpful...
modal.success_message: Request submitted! We'll track it as a GitHub issue.
modal.error_validation: Please fill in the adapter name and family.
